text.title: AI-Feature Observability Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the dashboard layout, metric names, and alert-rule defaults for the four pillars.

list.item: ai_request_latency_ms (histogram, p50/p95/p99 derived; labels: feature, model, route)
ai_request_cost_usd (histogram, breakdown labels: feature, model, direction in {input,cached_input,output,thinking})
ai_quality_score (gauge, 0-1 or 0-5; labels: feature, eval_set_id, run_id, stale: bool)
ai_drift_signal (gauge; labels: feature, drift_kind in {input_token_mean, refusal_rate, semantic_cluster}, baseline_window)

section.title: Dashboard required panels

list.item: Panel A: latency with p50/p95/p99 lines and SLO band shaded.
Panel B: cost histogram + cumulative-cost-today; tooltip breaks down by model and direction.
Panel C: quality_score time-series with `stale` flag overlay; eval-set run markers.
Panel D: drift_signal value with baseline distribution overlay; alert threshold line.

section.p: All four panels MUST be on a single page, visible together. Separate dashboards per pillar are REJECTED.

list.item: latency: page when p95 > SLO_band for 10 consecutive minutes.
cost: notify on Slack when daily cumulative cost exceeds baseline_daily_mean * 1.5; page when * 3.
quality: page when quality_score drops by >= 0.5 (on 0-5 scale) OR >= 0.1 (on 0-1 scale) versus baseline_mean; notify if `stale: true` for 2 consecutive runs.
drift: page when drift_signal exceeds 0.2 (KL or PSI) sustained over baseline_window.

antipattern.description: Cost dashboard expressed only in token counts.
antipattern.reason: r3 violation. Stakeholders cannot read tokens; currency is the floor.
r4 violation. Eval-set provenance is required.
r5 violation. Drift definition must be explicit.

antipattern.description: SLO band hardcoded from vendor docs ("p95 should be < 500ms").
antipattern.reason: r2 violation. SLO must derive from baseline traffic.

list.item: All four metric series emit data in the last 5 min.
SLO bands recorded in `slo-baseline.json` with baseline window.
eval_set_owner is a named individual, not a team.
Dashboard has all four panels on one page.
Alert rules wired to a paging channel, not just Slack.
